statements: - The deposition of sedimentary particles causes the formation of sedimentary rocks.
- The weathering of rocks leads to the formation of soil.
- The accumulation of organic matter causes the development of soil horizons.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Early Agricultural Practices
statements: The domestication of wild cereals causes the development of permanent settlements.
The adoption of irrigation techniques leads to increased agricultural productivity.
The transition to agriculture results in population growth.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Irrigation Systems and Water Management
statements: The development of complex irrigation systems causes increased agricultural productivity.
The construction of reservoirs leads to improved water storage and distribution.
The implementation of water management practices results in reduced soil erosion.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Urbanization and Settlement Patterns
statements: The development of irrigation systems causes increased agricultural productivity.
Increased agricultural productivity leads to population growth.
Population growth results in the formation of urban centers.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Technological Innovations in Farming
statements: The development of irrigation systems causes increased agricultural productivity.
The adoption of metal tools leads to more efficient land preparation.
The introduction of draft animals results in expanded cultivation areas.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Social and Economic Impacts
statements: The decline in monsoon rainfall causes agricultural failure.
Increased aridity leads to the abandonment of settlements.
The expansion of trade networks causes economic growth.

path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Environmental Changes and Human Adaptation
statements: The retreat of the Indian Summer Monsoon causes a decrease in agricultural productivity.
Increased aridity leads to a shift in settlement patterns.
A rise in sea level causes coastal site abandonment.

topic: Decline and Abandonment of Floodplain Settlements
path: Floodplain farming along the Indus and its tributaries
Decline and Abandonment of Floodplain Settlements
statements: Agricultural intensification causes soil salinization.
Soil salinization leads to crop failure.
Increased frequency of floods leads to abandonment of floodplain settlements.
